entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The fifth most important cereal crop globally, used for food, feed, fodder, and fuel

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: One of the top four important cereal crops in the world

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: One of the top four important cereal crops in the world

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: One of the top four important cereal crops in the world

entities.name: BARLEY
entities.description: One of the top four important cereal crops in the world

entities.name: FUEL CROP
entities.description: A crop grown specifically for the production of biofuels

entities.name: FOOD FEED AND FODDER UTILITIES
entities.description: The traditional uses of sorghum for human consumption and animal feed

entities.name: SELF-POLLINATION
entities.description: The predominant pollination mechanism in sorghum

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of developing new varieties to enhance crop characteristics

entities.name: OUTCROSSING
entities.description: The process of crossing between different genotypes, occurring at 5 to 15% in sorghum

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic constitution of an individual organism

entities.name: HUMIDITY
entities.description: An environmental factor that influences the rate of outcrossing in sorghum

entities.name: POPULATION IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: A breeding method used to exploit heterosis and improve crop populations

entities.name: HYBRID DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A breeding method used to exploit heterosis and create hybrid cultivars

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The phenomenon where hybrid offspring exhibit superior qualities compared to their parents

entities.name: GENETIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic condition that prevents pollen production, facilitating recurrent selection

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A form of male sterility involving interaction between cytoplasm and nucleus, facilitating hybrid development

entities.name: RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A population improvement procedure used to increase the frequency of desirable alleles
